If "Malapoenna nitens" is actually "Malpighia glabra", it is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to tropical America. The leaves and fruits of Malpighia glabra have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Malpighia glabra include flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made from Malpighia glabra include teas, infusions, and extracts, used to treat various health conditions, as well as for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
